name: Wagner Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: likely Mackinaw City Public Schools area (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No web presence identified; PO Box-only address in Mackinaw City, MI. No FMCSA SAFER snapshot match for this MI address (a Wagner Bus Service LLC exists in Butternut, WI - different company). Possibly an informal/very small or defunct operator.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~35 employees, ~$1.1M revenue) micro-operator in remote northern Michigan; no confirmable web/operating footprint. Too small and too poorly documented to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in; would require offline confirmation that the business is still operating.

name: Lake Crystal Sch Bus Svc Inc
owner_or_parent: Palmer Bus Service, Inc. (family-owned, Jenna Fromm CEO and Hollie Johnson co-owner; daughters of founders Floyd & Lois Palmer)
districts_or_customers: Lake Crystal Wellcome Memorial School District #2071 (~950 daily students; Lake Crystal, Butternut, Vernon Center, Rapidan, Judson, Garden City - Blue Earth County, MN)
recent_news: Legacy Stevenson-owned Lake Crystal Bus Service was sold/folded into Palmer Bus Service of Lake Crystal by 2021. Dudley Stevenson (former co-owner) died in 2022. Site managed by Mike Baker under Palmer brand. Palmer Bus Service is now the ~36th largest privately-run school bus company in the U.S., ~500+ buses, 28 MN districts; recognized by NSTA in 2024 for alternative fuel testing.
fit_notes: Not a standalone target - the Lake Crystal location is already a branch of Palmer Bus Service (family-owned, 28-district MN platform with ~500 buses). Palmer itself could be a Summit platform-tier consolidation play, but the Lake Crystal site alone is not actionable.

name: School Transportation Solutions
owner_or_parent: independent (privately held, locally-run; specific principals not publicly disclosed)
principals: L. Davis (contact email ldavis@sts-transportation.com - possible owner/exec)
districts_or_customers: central Alabama public and private schools
recent_news: No recent M&A or sale activity found. STS has operated continuously since 1996 (some sources say 1995) as a locally-run central Alabama transportation provider; main office at 3300 Rev. Abraham Woods Jr. Blvd., Birmingham, AL.
fit_notes: Genuinely independent, locally-owned central Alabama school transport contractor with ~30 years of operating history and mixed services (home-to-school, SPED, charter). Sub-scale (~$0.5M revenue/12 emp footprint per source data appears understated vs. an active multi-segment operator). Worth a phone-confirmation pass; could be a small AL tuck-in if fleet/contracts validate.

name: B & P Transit Inc
owner_or_parent: independent (estate / family succession from founder Pearl E. Cole)
principals: Owen W. Cole (son, Clayton DE - possible successor)
districts_or_customers: Smyrna School District (contracts for buses #6, 26, 28, 30 per 2025-26 contractor list)
recent_news: Founder Pearl E. Cole died November 2015 (age 87). Business has continued operating under B&P Transit name with multiple route contracts at Smyrna SD into 2025-26 school year - implying family succession (likely son Owen Cole). No public sale or PE acquisition recorded.
fit_notes: Small Delaware family-run school bus contractor (~4 routes for Smyrna SD), still operating ~10 yrs after founder's death. Strong succession signal post-founder; classic micro tuck-in profile, though scale is tiny (~$0.5M revenue, 12 employees). Useful if Summit is building a Delaware/Eastern Shore footprint via roll-up of small Smyrna-area contractors.

name: Kobussen Buses School Trnsprtn
districts_or_customers: DOD contracts (Department of Defense safety certified)
recent_news: Founded 1938 by Elwood 'Goldie' Kobussen; renamed Kobussen Buses Ltd. in 1976. 2018 - built new corporate HQ in Kaukauna for 80th anniversary; added 80+ propane-powered buses since 2014. 2012 School Bus Fleet Contractor of the Year. No M&A or sale activity found through 2025; remains family-owned across 20 locations.
fit_notes: Top-tier privately-held WI school bus operator (~900 employees, 20 locations, 28 districts, propane-forward fleet) - one of the largest independent contractors in the Midwest. Multi-generational family ownership (3rd gen actively involved) means succession signal is LOW near-term but this is a clear platform-grade target, not a tuck-in. Worth Summit BD relationship-building given scale and brand strength; would be a marquee acquisition rather than incremental tuck-in.
